action.hypothesis: Orthogonality regularization did not improve the K=128 TopK anchor, so the next within-family quality intervention is to train the K=128 model to remain reconstructive under slightly smaller nested supports; a lightly weighted matryoshka objective may sharpen feature ranking and improve full-K FVU.
action.summary: Run a proxy TopK experiment at K=128 with Adam and a mild matryoshka loss over smaller nested K values to test whether rank-aware training can beat the current TopK baseline.
action.self_review: This still relies on the current TopK family, so the upside may be limited. If the proxy remains flat or worse, that is a stronger signal that incremental TopK regularization is not enough and the next step should be a more substantial architecture implementation rather than another small training tweak.
notes_to_memory: TopK + orthogonality regularization failed to improve proxy quality at K=128, so simple decoder-basis regularization is not the missing ingredient.
Continue K=128 quality work with interventions aimed at better latent ranking or allocation rather than rewarding lower-K endpoints directly.
next_hypotheses: If TopK + matryoshka improves proxy quality, promote it and tune the nested K set or weights while keeping evaluation focused on K=128 quality.
If TopK + matryoshka is neutral or worse, stop incremental TopK regularizer search and implement a genuinely new architecture family with a sharper allocation mechanism.
If the matryoshka run helps quality but hurts throughput, keep it as a quality lead and follow with a targeted cost/perf cleanup later.
